segments.s651-g799: ز کشته is again 'from the slain'. به هر سو is a directional phrase 'toward every side / in every direction'. یکی توده بود is 'there was one heap' or idiomatically 'there lay a heap' — یکی here is indefinite ('a certain / a single') modifying توده (heap/mass) and بود is the past copula 'was'.
segments.s651-g800: گیاها is a collective plural 'plants/vegetation/grass'. به مغز سر is a locative/partitive phrase 'to/into the brain of the head' (مغز = brain; سر = head) describing where the plants were. آلوده بود is the adjectival-past 'was soiled/tainted/dirty' — together: 'plants were stuck in the brains of the heads and were soiled', a poetic way to describe grasses matted with (or smeared on) skulls/brains.
segments.s651-g137: زده is a past participle meaning “struck/afflicted” used predicatively; یک follows it as an indefinite/numeral (‘one/a certain’) in an inverted, poetic order. بر آن بد is a prepositional phrase ‘upon/by that bad (fortune)’, and که introduces a subordinate/result clause. کمتر کند uses کمتر (’less’) with کند (present/subjunctive/indicative ‘he makes/does’) to mean ‘makes less’ or ‘diminishes’.
segments.s651-g138: بکوشد is the (classical/historical) present/subjunctive form ‘he strives/tries’ (from کوشیدن). The following که here introduces the content/purpose clause. کهتر is a noun/adjective ‘the lesser, inferior’, چو is the simile/marker ‘like/as’, مهتر is an older title/adjective ‘the greater/chief’, and کند again is ‘he makes/causes’ (present). Together the clause expresses purpose/result: ‘he strives to make the inferior like the chief.’
segments.s651-g139: زمانه here means ‘fate/fortune’ rather than chronological ‘age’. ندادش is past negative ‘did not give him’ (ندادن + enclitic object -ش). بران is an adverbial/locative element ‘thereupon/there’, and بر درنگ is a preposition + noun ‘for delay/pause’ (درنگ = tarrying). The whole segment means ‘fortune gave him no time to linger there.’
segments.s651-g140: به دریا بس is an admonitory phrase: به + دریا ‘to/into the sea’ with بس ‘enough/be content’, i.e. ‘be satisfied with the sea’. ایمن مشو is a negative imperative/admonition (مشو, ‘do not become’), meaning ‘do not be secure/safe’. بر نهنگ is a prepositional phrase ‘on/upon a whale’ — a metaphorical warning not to trust an unstable perch. Note that مشو is 2nd-person negation but used generically as counsel.
segments.s651-g141: به کسری رسید is a simple past clause: به marks the recipient/goal (‘to Khosrow’), کسری is the name Khosrow (Kisra), and رسید is past ‘reached/arrived’. آن سزاوار تاج is a noun phrase with demonstrative آن ‘that’ modifying سزاوار ‘deserving/fit’ before the head noun تاج ‘crown’ (classical placement where the adjective precedes the noun).
segments.s651-g142: ببخشید here is past ‘he bestowed/gave’ (sense: to give/bestow). بر جای is a fixed prepositional phrase meaning ‘in place of / as a substitute’ (بر + جای), and ده یک خراج is an old fractional expression ‘one in ten (i.e. one‑tenth) of the kharāj’ where ده یک literally expresses the fraction and خراج is ‘tribute/tax’. The clause says he granted a tenth of the tribute in its stead.
segments.s651-g143: شدند is the past plural of شدن ‘they became/were made’. انجمن is the noun ‘assembly/meeting’. بخردان و ردان are two plural nouns in apposition meaning ‘the wise (بخردان)’ and ‘the righteous/ those who reject (ردان)’ — together they describe the members of the assembly. Note the classical/poetic word order where the verb precedes the noun phrase.
segments.s651-g144: بزرگان is ‘the great ones/older nobles’ and is joined by و (‘and’) to a noun phrase بیداردل موبدان where بیداردل (literally ‘wakeful‑hearted’) is an adjective modifying موبدان (mōbedān — Zoroastrian priests). The phrase names the distinguished participants: great men and vigilant priests.
segments.s651-g561: خردمند کسری is a nominative phrase with an epithet: 'the wise Khosrow' (کسری = Kisra, a royal name/title). چنان کرد رای is a past-tense verb phrase where چنان (so/such) modifies کرد (did/made) and رای (counsel/plan) is the object; literally 'he made such a counsel' = 'he devised such a plan.'
segments.s651-g562: کزان introduces a result/purpose clause ('from which' / 'so that'), followed by مرز as the subject. لختی is an adverb 'for a moment/a little while', بجنبد is the present/subjunctive form of جنبیدن ('to move/stir') used in a resultive sense, and ز جای is a prepositional phrase 'from its place.' The whole clause: 'from which the border would move a little from its place.'
segments.s651-g563: بگردد is a present/subjunctive or habitual-form of گشتن with the prefixed ب- (here 'will go about' / 'goes about'); یکی is an indefinite 'one/someone'; گرد + خرم + جهان is an adverbial phrase 'around the joyful world' (گرد = around/about, خرم = joyful, جهان = world). Word order places the verb first for poetic effect.
segments.s651-g564: گشاده کند is a verbal phrase: گشاده (open) combined with کند (does/makes) to mean 'makes open' → 'reveals/uncovers.' رازهای نهان is the direct object: رازهای (pl. 'secrets') modified by نهان ('hidden').
segments.s651-g565: بزد is simple past 'he struck' (here narrative past). کوس is the object 'war-drum.' وز is used poetically as an instrumental/ablative ('by/with the blast'), followed by جای لشکر ('the place of the host/army') and براند ('he drives/puts to flight') — a present-form used narratively for vividness meaning 'he drove the army from their place.'
segments.s651-g566: همی is an archaic/poetic particle (here emphatic 'indeed/so'); ماه و خورشید 'the moon and the sun' is the compound subject. ز + و (= ز او) functions as 'because of him / by him'; خیره ماند is a compound predicate: خیره (dazed/astonished) + ماند (remained) → 'remained astonished.'
segments.s651-g567: ز بس is a prepositional/causal phrase 'from/for so much' (ز = from, بس = so much/enough). It governs a series of coordinated nouns connected by و: پیکر (bodies), لشکر (army/hosts), سیم (silver, implied here among the coordinated items) and زر (gold). This is a nominative/ablative listing giving the source or abundance.
segments.s651-g568: کمرهای زرین is a noun phrase: کمرهای (plural 'girdles/belts') modified by the adjective زرین ('golden') — noun + adjective. Then و زرین سپر links 'and golden shields'; note poetic variation in adjective placement (زرین precedes سپر here). The phrase lists the valuable equipment resulting from the abundance.
segments.s651-g1297: سپاهی is the noun 'army/soldiers' functioning as the subject; بران is a locative/adverbial particle here 'upon that / on that (place)'; شهر names the place 'city'; شد is the past-tense copular/occurrence verb 'became/appeared' and takes the following predicate; بی‌کران is a predicative adjective 'countless'. Word order is subject + locative + verb + predicate (poetic inversion).
segments.s651-g1298: This is a nominal apposition specifying the composition of that host: دلیران is the plural adjective-noun 'the brave ones'; رومی qualifies them as 'of Rûm/byzantine'; و simply coordinates; کنداوران names another class of men. The whole phrase enumerates the troops that made up the host.
segments.s651-g1299: سه روز is a temporal phrase 'three days'. اندر(ان) here means 'there; in that place' (adverbial). شاه را uses the object/beneficiary marker را to indicate the king as the one affected. شد is the past verb 'there occurred/there was', and درنگ is the nominal predicate 'a delay / halt'. Together: 'A delay of three days occurred there for the king.'
segments.s651-g1300: بدان is a fixed adverbial phrase 'to that end / for that reason' (here written as ب + دان). تا introduces purpose 'so that / in order that'. نباشد is negative subjunctive/optative 'may not be / there be no' (classical negative mood after تا). به here marks the relation 'in/with regard to'; بیداد is the noun 'injustice/oppression'; جنگ is 'war'. The clause thus gives the purpose: 'so that there be no oppressive/unjust war.'
segments.s651-g1301: چهارم is a temporal adverbial 'on the fourth (day)'. سپاه is 'the army' (subject); اندر آمد is the compound verbal phrase 'came in / entered' (اندر = in/within + آمد = came); چو introduces a simile 'like/as'; کوه completes it 'a mountain' — 'the army came in like a mountain' (a poetic simile emphasizing mass).
segments.s651-g1302: This is a terse enumerative noun phrase: دلیران ایران = 'the brave men of Iran' (دلیران modifying ایران); گروها گروه is a repeating plural idiom meaning 'groups and groups / band after band' (گروها is the plural form, followed by the common noun گروه to intensify multiplicity). The phrasing lists the force's composition and multiplicity.
segments.s651-g1303: برفتند is the past plural verb 'they went / they rode off'. یک سر is an adverbial phrase here meaning 'all at once / as one body' (یک‌سر is an idiom meaning wholly/together); سواران روم is the subject 'the horsemen of Rûm' (Roman/Byzantine riders). Word order places the verb before the noun phrase for poetic effect.
segments.s651-g1304: ز بهر is a purposive prepositional phrase 'for the sake of / to take/for' (ز = from/poetic 'for', بهر = 'for/to'). The coordinated list زن و کودک و گنج و بوم gives the objects/purpose: 'women and children and treasure and the homeland' — things they sought to seize. The &-linked nouns enumerate what was taken or hunted.
segments.s651-g225: The phrase places the thing given first: به شاهی (‘to/into kingship’) followed by the object pronoun (مَرا, written here as م) + object marker را and the verb داد (past simple, 3rd sg. ‘gave’). یزدان پاک (the subject) comes after the verb for emphasis: ‘the Pure God gave me kingship.’ The preposition به + noun names the office/result conferred.
segments.s651-g226: A poetic range introduced by ز ... تا: ز خورشید تابنده (literally ‘from the shining sun’) تا تیره خاک (‘to the dark earth’). تابنده is a postpositive adjective modifying خورشید; تا here means ‘as far as / to’ indicating the spatial/figurative extent of the grant.
segments.s651-g227: نباید که introduces an impersonal prohibition/obligation (‘it should not be that…’). جز governs the excluded/only phrase: جز داد و مهر (‘except justice and kindness’). آوریم is 1st‑pl. present/subjunctive (we ought to/should bring/provide). داد here is a noun ‘justice’, not the verb.
segments.s651-g228: وگر = ‘and if / but if’ introduces a conditional. چین is a noun ‘wrinkle / frown’; به کاری is a prepositional phrase ‘in/to an action/deed’ (کاری here = ‘action, affair’), and بچهر آوریم combines بچهر (literally ‘to the face / upon the countenance’) with آوریم (1st‑pl. subjunctive/hypothetical ‘we cause/bring’): ‘but if we cause a frown to appear in some action.’
segments.s651-g229: Two nominal phrases juxtaposed as the protasis (condition): شبان بداندیش (‘an ill‑minded shepherd’) و دشت بزرگ (‘and a large plain’). There is no copula here; Persian often links such nouns/adjectives directly to imply a conditional situation for what follows.
segments.s651-g230: همی here is an archaic/adverbial particle marking ongoing or emphatic outcome (often like ‘will / indeed’). گوسفندان is the subject ‘the sheep’; بماند is a present/resultative verb ‘remain / be left’ (here ‘will be left’), and بگرگ is a contracted prepositional phrase ‘to the wolf’ (ب = به). Together: ‘the sheep will be left to the wolf.’
segments.s651-g231: نباید که again gives an impersonal prohibition (‘it should not be that…’). بر زیردستان ما uses بر + plural noun to mark the people affected (‘upon/against our subordinates’). The clause sets those who must not suffer the stated thing.
segments.s651-g232: ز introduces sources/agents (‘from’), repeated before the two groups: ز دهقان و ز دین‌پرستان ما (‘from the peasants and from our religious men’). Here وز represents the older combination و + ز (‘and from’). دین‌پرستان ما = ‘our religious people/zealots’.
segments.s651-g33: هر آنگه که introduces a conditional/time clause ('whenever' / 'every time that'). در کار سستی کنی literally reads 'you do laxity in the work' — سستی (laxity) is the object of کنی (the present/subjunctive form of کردن used here as a general/conditional 'you do' or 'you are'), so the clause means 'when/if you are lax in your work.' Word order is typical poetic Persian: subordinate clause introduced by که with the verb کنی at the end.
segments.s651-g34: همه رای ناتندرستی کنی is the main (apodotic) clause: همه رای ('every plan / all counsel') is the direct object and ناتندرستی is a predicative noun meaning 'unsoundness / feebleness'; کنی (the same present/subjunctive form of کردن) is the transitive verb 'you make / you cause.' So the structure is '[You] make every counsel unsound.'
segments.s651-g35: چو is a conditional temporal conjunction 'when.' چیره شود is a compound of the adjective چیره ('dominant, victorious') with the copula شود ('becomes') meaning 'becomes dominant / masters.' بر دل مرد is the prepositional phrase 'over a man's heart.' رشک (envy) is the semantic subject placed after the predicate for poetic emphasis, so the clause reads 'when envy masters a man's heart.'
segments.s651-g36: یکی دردمندی بود بی‌پزشک is an existential/predicative sentence. یکی ('a certain / one') introduces the existence of دردمندی ('the afflicted person' / 'the sufferer'). بود is the past copula 'was' (here used as a timeless narrative copula), and بی‌پزشک is a predicative complement 'without a physician.' Together: 'there was (or is) an afflicted person without a physician.'
segments.s651-g37: دل مرد is 'a man's heart/mind.' The adjectives/phrases بیکار و بسیار گوی follow and modify مرد in this poetic order: بیکار ('idle') and بسیار گوی ('much-speaking' / 'very talkative'). Note that گوی here functions as a deverbal/nominal element 'speaker' or 'speaking,' so the phrase describes an idle, talkative man.
segments.s651-g38: ندارد is the third-person present negative 'does not have.' به نزد کسان is a prepositional phrase meaning 'before / in the presence of people' (نزد = 'in the presence of'). آبروی is the object 'reputation / honor.' The whole clause states that such a man 'has no honor among people.'
segments.s651-g39: وگر is a conditional 'if.' بر خرد چیره گردد is 'becomes dominant over wisdom/reason' (چیره 'dominant' + گردد/گردد 'becomes'); هوا (desire/passion) is the subject placed after the verb phrase (poetic inversion). So the segment reads 'if desire gains the upper hand over reason.'
segments.s651-g40: نخواهد functions as a future/auxiliary with negative force here ('will not' / 'will not be inclined'); به دیوانگی is the directional/adverbial phrase 'toward madness / into madness.' بر گوا is a compact poetic phrase in which بر ('toward / upon') combines with گوا (here used adverbially as 'as/manifestly' or with a sense close to 'witness/manifestation'); the whole segment conveys that desire will not be kept in check and will show itself as madness. (Note: گوا is a poetic/archaic usage and its exact nuance here is adverbial/nominal 'as a manifestation/witness.')
segments.s651-g513: 'مبادا' is a literary negative optative ('may it not be' / 'heaven forbid'); 'که' introduces the subordinate clause. 'از' marks source ('from'), and 'کارداران من' = 'my managers/agents' (noun + possessor). The whole segment expresses a feared source: 'may it not be from my managers'.
segments.s651-g514: 'گر' is the conditional/contrastive particle 'if' (here functioning like 'or if'), followed by 'از' marking source. 'لشکر و پیشکاران من' means 'my army and my attendants/assistants' (coordinated nouns with the possessor 'من' following them). The phrase continues the feared sources introduced by 'مبادا'.
segments.s651-g515: 'نخسبد' is the negative present/subjunctive form meaning 'may not sleep' or imperatively 'let ... not sleep' in the third person. With the negation it makes 'کسی' function as 'no one'. 'با دلی دردمند' is a prepositional phrase 'with a sorrowful/grieving heart' where 'دلی' ('a heart') is modified by 'دردمند'.
segments.s651-g516: 'که' introduces the feared result ('lest / that'); 'از درد او' means 'from his suffering' (source of the consequence). 'بر من آید' is a present/subjunctive construction meaning 'may come upon me' (آید here expresses the feared potential), and 'گزند' is the harm/affliction that may befall the speaker.
segments.s651-g517: 'سخنها' is the plural 'words' or 'things said'. 'اگرچه بود' is a concessive past construction 'even if there were' (اگرچه = 'although / even if'; بود = 'were'). 'در نهان' is the locative phrase 'in secret' or 'hidden away'.
segments.s651-g518: 'بپرسد' (verb with the prefix ب-) here has a future/present sense 'will ask' or 'asks' in third person; Persian poetry often places the verb before its subject. 'ز من' = 'of/from me' (i.e., 'ask me'), and 'کردگار جهان' is the subject 'the Creator/Lord of the world' which follows the verb.
segments.s651-g519: 'ز' is the poetic form of 'از' meaning 'about/from'. 'باژ و خراج آن' names the items in question ('baž' and its tribute/tax), with 'آن' = 'that/its'. 'کجا مانده است' is a rhetorical question 'where has it remained / where has it gone', using مانده (past participle) + است.
segments.s651-g520: 'که' introduces the content of the question ('that ...'). 'موبد' is the mōbed (Zoroastrian priest). 'به دیوان ما' is a prepositional phrase 'into/into the diwan/register of ours' (diwan here = register/record), and 'رانده است' is a perfect form 'has driven/put' (رانده + است = 'has put/has been driven'), describing the priest's action recorded in the diwan.
segments.s651-g529: 'برفتند' is a past tense plural verb (they went/they departed); the subject is omitted (understood 'they'). The prepositional phrase 'با شادی و خرمی' (
segments.s651-g530: The clause begins with the simile marker 'چو' (= 'as/like'), followed by the simile noun 'باغ ارم' (the Garden of Iram, here a proper name). 'گشت' is the past intransitive 'became/turned'; 'روی زمی' is a nominal phrase 'face (of) the earth' where 'روی' = face and 'زمی' = 'earthly/pertaining to the earth' (poetic order). The whole means 'the earth's face became like the Garden of Iram.'
segments.s651-g531: The preposition 'ز' (= 'from') marks source. 'گیتی' = 'the world'. 'ندیدی' is a past negative of 'دیدن' used impersonally/general — 'one did not see' or 'you did not see'; 'کسی را' is the direct-object phrase ('anyone' with the object marker 'را'), and 'دژم' is a predicative adjective 'sullen/grim' describing that object. Word order places the source phrase first for emphasis.
segments.s651-g532: 'ز ابر' = 'from the clouds' (source), 'اندر آمد' is a two-part verbal expression meaning 'came forth/appeared' (اندر used with آمد in classical/poetic usage). 'به هنگام نم' is a temporal phrase 'at the time of the moisture/dew' (به + هنگام = 'at the time of'). The clause describes moisture arising from the clouds at dew-time.
segments.s651-g533: جهان شد is the subject 'the world' plus شد 'became'. به کردار is a prepositional phrase 'in the manner/likeness of' (به + کردار, where کردار functions as 'manner/behavior'). خرم بهشت is a noun phrase 'joyful paradise' with خرم as an adjective modifying بهشت; together they state that the world assumed the appearance of a joyous paradise.
segments.s651-g534: This clause is a condensed, poetic nominal construction: ز باران هوا 'the air from/by rain' (ز = 'from/because of', هوا = 'air/atmosphere') acts as agent, بر زمین is the locative 'upon the earth' (بر = on/upon), and لاله کشت literally 'tulip cultivation' — here used elliptically to mean 'tulips were sown/grew'. The overall sense is that because of the rains the air caused tulips to be on the earth.
segments.s651-g535: در و دشت و پالیز is a simple list of places ('gates/doors, plains, and orchards'); در here is the noun 'door/gate' not the preposition. شد is the past/resultative 'became', and چون چراغ is a simile 'like a lamp' modifying the whole list. Word order (noun list + شد + چون + simile noun) is typical of Persian poetic inversion.
segments.s651-g536: چو خورشید شد باغ places the simile before the verb: 'the garden became as the sun' (چو/چو = 'as/like', شد = 'became', باغ = 'garden'). The second half, و چون ماه راغ, omits a repeated verb (elliptical) and supplies a second simile 'and like the moon of longing' — راغ here is an attributive/poetic noun 'longing/desire', so 'ماه راغ' reads as 'moon of desire' or 'a moon inspiring longing'.
segments.s651-g705: همه is the subject meaning “everyone.” پیش here is a preposition meaning “in the presence of / before” and governs the following noun phrase نوشین‌روان, which is an epithet used like a proper name (“sweet‑souled”). آمدند is the third‑person plural past tense (“they came”). Word order: subject + prepositional phrase + verb.
segments.s651-g706: ز introduces source or cause (“from”); کار گذشته is a noun phrase ‘the past affair/deeds’ (کار = affair/deed, گذشته = past). نوان is an archaic/poetic adjective/adverb here meaning “new/renewed,” and آمدند is the same past plural verb “they came,” so the phrase says they arrived renewed as a result of what had passed.
segments.s651-g707: چو is a temporal conjunction meaning “when.” پیش again = “before/in the presence of.” سراپرده شهریار is a genitive/attributive phrase “the king’s inner chamber/curtain” (سراپرده = inner chamber, شهریار = king). Together the segment sets the time/place for the following verb.
segments.s651-g708: رسیدند is the past plural of رسیدن “they arrived/reached.” با هدیه و با نثار uses repeated با “with” to list what they bore: هدیه and نثار are offerings/tributes (نثار here functions as a noun ‘offering’). The structure is straightforward: they arrived with gift and with offering.
segments.s651-g709: خروشان and غلتان are present‑participle/gerund forms used adverbially (“roaring” / “rolling, tumbling”) describing their manner. به introduces the direction/location (“onto/into”), خاک here emphasizes the dust/ground, and اندرون means the inner quarters/inside; together: they tumbled roaring into the inner floor.
segments.s651-g710: همه “all” is the subject. دیده پر خاک is a noun + predicate pattern: دیده “eyes” + پر “full (of)” + خاک “dust,” i.e. “eyes full of dust.” دل پر ز خون uses پر with a following z‑phrase (ز خون) to indicate what fills the heart: “heart full of blood.” The coordinating و links the two descriptions.
segments.s651-g711: خرد “wisdom” is the subject; چون is the temporal conjunction “when.” بود is the past tense “was.” با دلاور indicates association/possession (“with the brave man/hero”), and به راز is an adverbial phrase “in secret” (به + راز) modifying the state ‘was.’ The clause means wisdom resided secretly with the hero.
segments.s651-g712: به شرم و به پوزش are prepositional phrases literally “for shame and for apology.” نیاید is negative present of آمدن here with idiomatic force “does not arise / there is no need,” and نیاز is the noun “need.” Together the idiom نیاید نیاز means “no need arises” — i.e., shame or apology are unnecessary.
segments.s651-g385: یکی here is an indefinite particle 'a certain/one (man)', followed by the noun phrase 'گرزه گاوپیکر' (a mace described as cow/ox‑shaped). The postposition/particle phrase 'به چنگ' attaches as an instrumental/locational phrase meaning 'in (his) grip/hand' (چنگ = claw/hand). Word order is noun modifiers following the head ('گرزه' + adjective phrase 'گاوپیکر'), and 'یکی' introduces the unspecified person.
segments.s651-g386: زده بر here is a past participle/verb 'fastened/struck on' used to indicate something attached to the body; 'بر' = 'on/upon' and 'کمرگاه' = 'waist/small of the back'. The two nouns 'تیر خدنگ' follow as the things attached (an arrow and a short sword/dagger). Grammatically, the verb/participle governs the following locative phrase.
segments.s651-g387: 'به بازو کمان' is a simple locative phrase 'a bow on (his) arm' (به = on/at). The conjunction 'و' links a parallel phrase 'بزین بر کمند' in which بزین functions as 'saddled/with a saddle' (participial/ adjectival use) and 'بر کمند' specifies location 'on the girth/strap'. The second 'به' is omitted by parallelism; the structure lists the equipment on body locations.
segments.s651-g388: 'میان را' marks 'the middle/midriff' as the definite object (را). 'بزرین کمر' is a noun phrase 'a broad/large belt', where بزرین = 'great/broad' modifies کمر = 'belt/waistband'. 'کرده بند' is a verbal/participial cluster meaning 'had bound/fastened' (کرده = made/having made; بند = band/binding). Word order is somewhat inverted but expresses 'he had bound a broad belt about his middle.'
segments.s651-g389: برانگیخت اسب is a past transitive clause 'he roused/spurred the horse' (برانگیخت = roused). The conjunction 'و' links the next verb phrase 'بیفشارد ران' in which بیفشارد is a past verb 'he pressed/pressed on' and ران names the flanks/thighs (here the horse's flanks) — together meaning he urged the horse on with his legs.
segments.s651-g390: 'به گردن برآورد' is a light-verb construction meaning 'he put/hoisted upon the neck/shoulder' (به گردن = upon the neck; برآورد is the past of برآوردن 'to bring/put (up)'). The nominal phrase 'گرز گران' 'a heavy mace' is the object being placed. The clause thus means he shouldered a heavy mace.
segments.s651-g391: 'عنان را' marks 'the reins' as the definite object (را). 'چپ و راست' is a two-part adverbial 'to the left and right' (i.e., he moved them both ways). 'لختی' is an adverb 'for a short while' and 'بسود' ('به سود' contracted) means 'to advantage' or 'for effect' — together: he flicked the reins left and right briefly to show off or to good effect.
segments.s651-g392: 'سلیح سواری' is a compound noun 'armed rider/armored horseman' (سلیح = weapon, سواری = rider). The verb 'نمود' is the past form 'showed/displayed/appeared', and 'به بابک' marks the indirect object/beneficiary 'to Babak' — so the phrase means the armed horseman displayed himself (his arms and riding) to Babak.
segments.s651-g1377: ز is a causal/prepositional preface 'because of' linking the following epithet to the result. نوشین‌روان is an epithet used as a noun 'the sweet‑souled one'. شد is the past/copulative 'became' and takes دلش (دل + enclitic ش 'his') as its subject; پر هراس is a predicative phrase 'full of fear'. Word order (cause phrase before verb) is typical of classical Persian narrative.
segments.s651-g1378: همی is a frequentative/adverb meaning 'continually/keeps on'. رای زد is an idiom: رای 'counsel/plan' plus زدن (زد) ‘to strike/produce’ = 'to devise/turn over a plan'. روز و شب 'day and night' marks habitual time. در سه‌پاس is a manner phrase 'while in/with three paces' (literal 'in three‑paces'), describing how he paced as he brooded. The past form زد with همی conveys repeated action in epic narrative.
segments.s651-g1379: بدو is the prepositional phrase 'to him' (word order: indirect object before verb, common in older Persian). گفت here marks reported speech 'said' (the lemma list gives noun senses but syntactically functions as the speech verb). موبد is the speaker 'the mōbed'. که introduces the content clause 'that/that this is...' and این رای نیست is the propositional content 'this counsel is not (right)'.
segments.s651-g1380: The second که functions causally 'for/because' (giving the reason). با رزم کسری 'in/against the battle of Khosrow' uses با + noun to mean 'in the face of' or 'against'. تو را uses the clitic object marker را after the pronoun for emphasis, and پای نیست is a predicative negation 'there is no footing/you have no standing' (پای 'foot/footing', نیست 'is not').
segments.s651-g1381: برآرند is a third‑person plural verbal form meaning 'they take/bring away' (here future/prospective sense in narrative). ازین is a contracted from 'از این' 'from this'; مرز آباد 'this prosperous/blooming border' is the source, and خاک 'soil/earth' is the object being removed. The verb placed before its object is common in the elevated style.
segments.s651-g1382: شود کرده is a periphrastic construction: شود (become) + کرده (made/doing) yields a passive/result sense 'will be made' or 'will be cast'. قیصر is the subject (placed after the verb, as often in Persian poetry). اندر مغاک 'into the abyss' gives the destination. Together: 'the Caesar will be made/laid in the gulf/abyss.'
segments.s651-g1383: زوان is 'speech/words' (here 'the words/voice'), سراینده is 'the singer/poet' (the agent of those words). و simply joins the two coordinated items. رای سست is a noun + adjective 'feeble counsel' — رای 'counsel/plan' modified by سست 'weak/lax'. The phrase lists the inadequate elements being criticized.
segments.s651-g1384: جز از ... نجست is an exceptive construction 'sought nothing except from ...' — جز from the start functions as 'except/only'. از رنج 'by/from hardship' gives the means, بر پادشاهی 'for/to the kingship' is the goal (بر here = 'toward/for'), and نجست (here the verb 'sought/obtained') is past‑tense 'sought' (sense 'to seek'). The overall meaning: such words and weak counsel could attain the throne only through suffering.
segments.s651-g1329: Poetic, somewhat inverted word order: نگه کرد باید = باید نگه کرد ('one must guard/watch'). نگه کرد is a compound verb here meaning 'to keep/guard' (past-stem used in the periphrasis). بدین is the prepositional phrase 'به + این' = 'over/to this', followed by تازه بوم 'this new land'.
segments.s651-g1330: که introduces purpose/optative here ('so that / in order that'). آباد is an adjectival predicate 'prosperous/inhabited', بادا is an optative particle 'may it be', and همه مرز روم = 'all the frontiers/borders of Rum (the Romans/Byzantium)'.
segments.s651-g1331: یکی شهر فرمود = 'he caused/ordered a certain city (to be made/established)'. یکی here = 'a certain', شهر = 'city', فرمود is used in the sense 'he commanded/caused' (classical usage for founding or ordering), and نوشین روان is a two-word descriptive phrase 'pleasant/sweet and flowing' modifying شهر.
segments.s651-g1332: بدو = به او / 'to it' (dative pronoun pointing back to the city). اندرون آبهای روان literally 'inside, (there were) flowing waters' — اندرون gives the locative 'within', آبهای = 'waters', روان = 'flowing'. The verb 'were' is omitted (a common poetic ellipsis).
segments.s651-g1333: به کردار انطاکیه = 'in the manner/appearance of Antioch' (به ... کردار = 'in the fashion of'). چون چراغ = 'like a lamp' — a simile; چراغ modifies the preceding phrase to say the city shone/appeared like a lamp. Word order places the simile directly after the manner phrase for emphasis.
segments.s651-g1334: پر از = 'full of' (idiomatic). A list follows (گلشن و کاخ و میدان و باغ) connected by و; each item is a noun describing what the place was full of: flower-gardens, palaces, public squares, and orchards/gardens.
segments.s651-g1335: بزرگان = 'the great ones' (nobles/elders). روشن‌دل و شادکام are coordinate adjectives 'bright‑hearted / clear‑souled and happy/fortunate' describing those people; adjectives follow the noun in apposition.
segments.s651-g1336: ورا = 'to it' (dative). زیب خسرو is the name assigned (a name phrase without an explicit ezāfe in this poetic line: 'Zib‑e Khosrow' / 'the beauty/ornament Khosrow'). نهادند نام = 'they placed/assigned the name' (نهادند = past plural 'they set/placed', نام = 'name') — together 'they gave it the name ...'.
segments.s651-g449: به دل گفتم is a simple past clause 'I said to my heart' (به + دل + گفتم). آرم here is a short, archaic/poetic first-person form introducing what the speaker resolves — read as an optative/future-like declaration ('I will...' / 'be at rest' depending on punctuation). ز هر سو سپاه is a prepositional/partitive phrase 'an army from every side/direction' (ز = 'from', هر سو = 'every side', سپاه = 'army') that completes the announced intention.
segments.s651-g450: بخواهم is a present/future-oriented desire ('I will seek / I shall want'); the verb governs the following source phrase. ز هر کشوری is 'from every country/realm' with ز indicating source. رزمخواه is a compound noun 'one who desires battle' or 'fighter' and is the object sought.
segments.s651-g451: نگردد is a negated present/jussive form meaning 'does not turn/does not come together' (literary). سپاه انجمن is a noun phrase 'the army's assembly' or 'the host's muster' (two nouns in apposition). جز به گنج = 'except for treasure' (جز = except, به گنج = for/ because of treasure) expresses the motive or cause for the muster.
segments.s651-g452: به بی مردی is a causal/locative phrase 'by/with lack of manliness' (بی = without, مردی = manliness). آید is the present tense 'comes' or 'befalls'. هم is an emphatic/additive 'also'. از گنج رنج = 'sorrow/trouble from treasure' (از = from, گنج = treasure, رنج = suffering), i.e. wealth brings trouble if there is no manliness.
segments.s651-g453: اگر introduces a conditional 'if'. بد here functions as a substantive/adverbial 'ill/misfortune' (i.e., 'if misfortune'). به درویش خواهد رسید is a light-future construction: به = 'to/upon', درویش = 'the dervish/poor ascetic', خواهد + رسید = 'will reach / will befall' (classical future sense).
segments.s651-g454: ازین آرزو = 'from this desire' (از + این, here spelled ازین, + آرزو). دل بباید برید is an obligation construction: دل = 'the heart', بباید = 'one must / ought to' (present modal), برید is the infinitive/past-stem of بریدن 'to cut/tear away' (here 'to sever/abandon'); together 'the heart must be torn away (from this desire).' Note: برید here is used in the sense 'to sever/tear off'.
segments.s651-g455: همی‌راندم is an imperfect/continuous past 'I was turning over / I repeatedly turned' (literary habitual). با دل خویش is a possessive phrase 'with my own heart' (با = with, دل = heart, خویش split as خوی + ش = 'own'/'his own'). راز is the direct object 'the secret' — the speaker is repeatedly turning this secret over in his heart.
segments.s651-g456: چو = 'when'. اندیشه پیش خرد = 'thought before wisdom' or 'thought in front of/before reason' (پیش = before/in front of, خرد = wisdom/reason). شد فراز is a compound where شد = 'became' and فراز = 'on high/rising' (literary 'rose up' / 'was elevated'), so the clause reads 'when thought rose above wisdom' or more literally 'when thought became lofty before wisdom.'
segments.s651-g1265: همه پاک زین شهر بیرون شوید is an imperative address to a group. همه ('everyone') is the vocative/subject, پاک here is an adverb meaning 'completely/at once' modifying the command, زین is an archaic/poetic contraction (literally z + in) meaning 'from this' and precedes شهر ('city'); بیرون شوید is the 2nd‑person plural/formal imperative or hortative form built on شدن (here ‘go/leave, become outside’), so the clause literally orders everyone to become outside of this city.
segments.s651-g1266: This is a pair of prepositional/locative phrases giving the destination: به تاریکی اندر is 'into/unto the darkness' (به + noun, with اندر reinforcing 'within'), and به هامون شوید repeats the verb from the previous segment (imperative plural) to mean 'enter/go into the plain (hâmun)'. The doubled preposition pattern (به ... اندر) is poetic emphasis on motion into darkness.
segments.s651-g1267: اگر introduces a conditional ('if'). هیچ modifies بانگ and functions here as 'any'/'whatever' (poetic use), so the phrase بانگ زن و مرد پیر reads as 'a cry (from) woman or (from) man, (from) an elder' with the ezāfe relation omitted for metrical reasons; the sequence lists possible criers (زن و مرد پیر).
segments.s651-g1268: وگر is an alternative conditional particle ('and if' / 'but if') that begins a second set of possibilities. The nouns غارت، شورش، داروگیر are simply enumerated (plunder, uprising/tumult, and داروگیر — here a noun listed without an explicit verb); the verb is left understood (ellipsis of 'آید' or 'شود'), a common poetic device.
segments.s651-g1269: به گوش من آید is a dative construction ('it comes to my ear' / 'I hear'); به + noun (به گوش) marks the experiencer, من is the possessor/agent in the dative phrase, and آید is the impersonal/personal present tense 'comes' used to report hearing. بتاریک شب is a temporal adverbial ('in the dark night') placed after the verb for poetic order.
segments.s651-g1270: که introduces reported content ('that ...'). بگشاید is a 3rd‑person singular present/subjunctive form meaning 'will open/relieve' here used causatively/optatively ('will relieve'); از رنج marks the source ('from suffering'), and یک مردلب is an indefinite/poetic way to name the person affected ('a certain Mardlab' or 'one called Mardlab').
segments.s651-g1271: هم اندر زمان آنک sets the temporal frame ('also, at that time'), with آنک functioning as a relative/conjunctive 'that/when'. فریاد ازوست (poetic contraction of از اوست) says 'the cry is from him' — ازوست is an archaic/poetic fused form meaning 'is from him'. Note the inversion and compressed syntax typical of epic verse.
segments.s651-g1272: پر از کاه بینند آگنده پوست is a visual clause: پر از کاه ('full of straw') modifies پوست ('skins'), and بینند is the 3rd‑person plural present 'they see'. The adjective آگنده ('filled', 'laden') reinforces the state of the skins; word order is inverted for metrical/poetic effect (attribute phrases placed before and after the verb).
segments.s651-g977: ز is the poetic preposition 'from' marking origin. دشت (plain) precedes سواران (horsemen) in inverted/poetic order to mean 'horsemen of the plain', and نیزه‌وران is an appositive/compound describing those horsemen as 'spearmen' or 'lancers'.
segments.s651-g978: برآریم is a first‑person plural hortative/subjunctive ('let us raise' / 'we will raise') used to propose action. گرد here functions adverbially as 'around/throughout' (a mass/host sense in context). از کران تا کران is a range expression 'from edge to edge' with از ... تا marking the limits.
segments.s651-g979: نه is the negative particle 'nor/not'. خورشید ('the sun') is the grammatical subject and نوشین‌روان ('sweet‑souled' / 'pleasant‑minded') is the noun phrase describing the created being. آفرید is the simple past 'created' (here negated by نه).
segments.s651-g980: وگر introduces a concessive/conditional 'and if / but if'. بستد is a third‑person verbal form meaning 'shuts / should shut' (used here with a conditional sense). از چرخ گردان کلید is a genitival/source phrase 'the key of the revolving wheel (the heaven)', where گردان is the adjectival 'revolving' and کلید is used figuratively.
segments.s651-g981: که introduces the subordinate/explanatory clause 'that / who'. کس را uses را as the definite object marker ('no one' as object), and نخواند is the negative present (archaic/poetic) form 'does not call / will not call'; همی is an adverb 'always/ever'. از مهان means 'from the guests' (i.e. of the gathered/noble ones).
segments.s651-g982: همه modifies کام to mean 'every/entire desire'. او is the third‑person subject 'he', and یابد is a present tense verb 'finds/attains' (here conveying 'he attains'). اندر جهان is the locative phrase 'in the world'.
segments.s651-g983: فرستاده را is the indirect object/topic 'to the messenger' (را marks it as definite/affected). هیچ + noun with a past negative verb yields 'no ...'; پاسخ نداد is 'gave no answer' — نداد is داد with the negative prefix ن, i.e. 'did not give'.
segments.s651-g984: به تندی is an adverbial phrase 'with haste / quickly'. ز کسری ('from Khosrow') marks source/mention. نیامدش is a compound where the enclitic pronoun ش (to him) attaches to نیامد 'did not come'; یاد ('thought/memory') thus 'no thought of Khosrow came to him' = 'he did not recall Khosrow'.
segments.s651-g905: ز درگاه is an adverbial prepositional phrase (ز = 'from') giving the source — 'from the portal/royal court'. برخاست is a past simple verb 'rose' (برخاستن) which takes the following noun phrase آوای کوس ('the sound/cry of the drum') as what rose; word order is slightly inverted for poetic effect (prepositional phrase + verb + subject/object).
segments.s651-g906: This is two short predications expressing change: زمین قیرگون شد 'the earth became pitch-black' (زمین = subject, قیرگون = predicate adjective, شد = past of شدن 'become'). هوا آبنوس repeats the pattern elliptically (the verb شد is omitted and understood): 'the air (became) ebony.' The adjective follows the noun and the verb شدن marks the change.
segments.s651-g907: گزین کرد is a compound past ('chose/selected') where گزین is the lexical verb and کرد functions as the past auxiliary. زان introduces the source clause 'from that' and لشکر نامدار is the noun phrase 'that famed army/host' with the adjective نامدار postposed, a typical poetic order.
segments.s651-g908: سواران شمشیرزن is a compound noun phrase 'mounted swordsmen' (سواران 'horsemen' modified by شمشیرزن 'swordsmen'); the numeral سی‌هزار follows the noun phrase and quantifies it — classical/poetic word order often places numerals after the counted noun for rhythm.
segments.s651-g909: به منذر سپرد is a verb phrase 'entrusted to Munḏir' with به indicating the indirect object/recipient (to Munḏir). آن سپاه گران is the direct object 'that heavy/mighty army' (آن determiner + سپاه 'army' + postpositive adjective گران). The verb سپرد (past of سپردن) governs this object and recipient.
segments.s651-g910: بفرمود introduces reported speech/command 'he ordered'. کز begins the subordinate clause (here functioning like 'that' or 'from which/of which') and دشت نیزه‌وران 'the spearmen from the plain' names the source/group intended; the full clause continues after this segment. Note بفرمود is an honorific/polite speech verb used to report an order.
segments.s651-g911: سپاهی بر از جنگجویان بروم is a short clause commanding or wishing that a force go forth: سپاهی 'a troop/force', بر functions adverbially here to indicate motion forward or out, از جنگجویان 'from among the warriors', and بروم is the present-subjunctive form of رفتن ('go') used to express the desired action. The subject is the troop (سپاهی) and the verb is subjunctive, giving a purposive/command sense after the previous بفرمود.
segments.s651-g912: که introduces a purpose/result clause 'so that / in order that'. آتش برآرند uses the present-subjunctive third-person plural برآرند ('they may bring/raise') with آتش as its object — here 'set fire' or 'bring fire'. زان مرز و بوم is the target 'that border and land' (زان as a demonstrative/relational marker + مرز 'border' و بوم 'and land'), completing the purpose: that they set that border and country on fire.
segments.s651-g1289: بران is an adverb meaning 'thereupon' or 'on that'; it sets the temporal link to what just happened. رومیان is the (plural) subject 'the Romans'. بر here is the preposition marking the object (toward/to), and ببخشود is a 3rd-person past verb 'forgave/pardoned' with an archaic/epic -ود ending; classical narrative sometimes uses a singular verb form where modern Persian expects plural agreement. شاه is the object 'the king'.
segments.s651-g1290: گنهکار is an adjectival/nominative phrase 'guilty' or 'accused'. شد is the past-tense copular/auxiliary 'became/was'. رسته is a past-participle/adjective 'freed, released (from blame)' used predicatively with شد. و links a coordinate adjective, and بیگناه is 'innocent'. Together this says he 'was freed and (thus) innocent'.
segments.s651-g1291: بسی modifies خواسته as an intensive/quantifying adverb/adjective ('much/many'); خواسته is a noun 'wish/request'. پیش ایشان is a prepositional phrase 'before/with them' (پیش = in the presence of/with), and بماند is a 3rd-person subjunctive/present-stem used in narrative meaning 'remained' or 'was left'. The whole segment says that many requests remained with them.
segments.s651-g1292: وزان is a connective adverb 'and/from that (place or time)' linking to what follows. جایگه is 'place/position' and نیز 'also'; لشکر is the noun 'army'. براند is a present/past narrative form of راندن 'to drive/send forth' (here 'sent/put in motion'). The clause means that from that place they likewise sent the army.
segments.s651-g1293: هران کس که بود is a formulaic relative 'whoever/anyone who was'. هران (= هر آن) = 'each/any', کس = 'person', که introduces the relative clause, and بود is the past copula 'was'. از در کارزار is a prepositional phrase 'from/in the field of battle' (در = in/at, کارزار = battlefield); together it restricts 'whoever was at the battle'.
segments.s651-g1294: ببستند is a past plural 'they bound/tied' (here: strapped onto). بر + پیل = 'on the elephant(s)'. و is the coordinator 'and'. کردند is past plural 'they made/did' and بار is 'load/burden', so کردند بار = 'they loaded' or 'made (them) a load'. The sequence describes fastening people/equipment onto elephants and loading them.
segments.s651-g1295: به انطاکیه در is a locative phrase 'in/at Antioch' (به + place, with در reinforcing location). خبر شد is an impersonal/narrative expression 'it became news' = 'news arrived' or 'word came'. ز شاه is a source phrase 'from the king'. The segment therefore means 'in Antioch there came news from the king.'
segments.s651-g1296: که introduces the content clause 'that ...'. با پیل و لشکر is an instrumental/companion phrase 'with elephants and (with) an army'. بیامد is the past-tense 3rd-person 'he came / was coming'. به راه is an adverbial 'on the road / along the way'. Together: 'that he came/was coming with elephants and an army along the road.'
segments.s651-g401: عنان را places 'reins' as a definite direct object with را. یکی functions adverbially here ('once' / 'one time' or 'with one movement'). بازپیچی is the action 'twist/turn back' presented as the verb/imperative-like command in this poetic line. براست is an adverbial modifier (here directional/straight/rightward) that qualifies how to twist the reins.
segments.s651-g402: چنان introduces a result/comparison clause ('so, in such a way'). کز is the classical complementizer/relative 'that/which' linking the clause. هنرمندی is an abstract noun 'skill/artistry' and is linked syntactically to تو ('your') as its possessor. سزاست is a fused verbal phrase ('it is fitting / it is deserved') in the third singular present/presentive, asserting that the action befits the addressee's skill.
segments.s651-g403: دگرباره is an adverb 'again / anew' modifying the clause. کسری is the proper-name subject (Khosrow). برانگیخت is a past, transitive verb 'roused/raised' (3rd person singular past) taking اسب as its object; the poetic word order places elements compactly but the structure is subject–verb–object (with the adverb upfront).
segments.s651-g404: چپ و راست is a coordinated adverbial phrase 'to the left and to the right'. برسان is an imperative form (2nd person) of رساندن 'bring/send/dispatch', here giving an order. آذرگشسب follows as the object of برسان (poetic word order allows object after verb); the whole segment is an imperative instruction to dispatch that hero in both directions.
segments.s651-g405: نگه کرد is a light verbal construction literally 'made/look' used as a past tense 'he looked'. بابک is the subject (inserted after the verb for emphasis). ازو is a prepositional pronominal form 'at/from him' (classical Persian) marking the target of the gaze. خیره ماند is a combination where خیره (staring/astonished) is a predicative adjective/participle and ماند is the past 'remained' — together 'remained astonished / stood staring'.
segments.s651-g406: جهان‌آفرین را uses را to mark 'the Creator' as the direct object. فراوان is an adverbial modifier 'frequently/abundantly'. بخواند is a literary past-form of خواندن used here in the sense 'call upon / invoke / recite' (third-person singular), so the clause means he repeatedly called on the Creator.
segments.s651-g407: سواری هزار is a noun phrase 'a rider of a thousand' (hyperbolic epithet). و is the coordinating 'and'. گوی here is the quotative/parenthetical 'they say' (classical usage) introducing the reported number دوهزار ('two thousand') as an alternative estimate.
segments.s651-g408: نبودی is the negative past existential 'there was not / there did not exist' (3rd singular). کسی را places 'anyone' as a topicalized/affected object (را for emphasis). گذر بر چهار is a nominal phrase 'a passing/passage beyond four' where بر introduces the measure 'over/further than four' — the whole line asserts that no one had a passage/score that exceeded four.
segments.s651-g41: بکژی (here) functions as a predicative verb/adjective meaning 'be crooked/askew' or 'go wrong'; it predicates the state of the following object. تو را is the ethical/dative object 'to you/for you' (تو + object marker را), and راه نزدیکتر is the noun phrase 'the nearer road/way' (راه modified by comparative نزدیکتر). Word order is poetic: predicate + indirect object + noun phrase, so the clause reads roughly 'the nearer road for you is crooked.'
segments.s651-g42: سوی راستی is a prepositional phrase 'toward (the) uprightness/truth' (سوی = toward(s); راستی = uprightness/truth). راه باریکتر is a noun phrase 'the narrower road' (راه + comparative باریکتر). In poetry the two noun phrases are juxtaposed to state: 'the way toward uprightness is narrower.'
segments.s651-g43: به کاری is a prepositional noun phrase 'in/for a matter/undertaking' (به = to/for; کاری = 'a matter' here). کزو = 'from which' / 'of which' links that matter to the action that follows. پیشدستی کنی is a present/subjunctive or generic 2nd-person clause 'you take precedence / you take the initiative' (پیشدستی = initiative; کنی = you do/you take). Together: 'if you take the initiative in some matter.'
segments.s651-g44: به آید که is an impersonal clause 'it may happen that' (به آید = 'it comes/happens that'; که introduces the subordinate clause). کندی و سستی کنی places responsibility on the subject (here: 'you') with کنی 'you do/you become' and the nouns کندی (slowness) و سستی (laxity): 'that you (will) be slow and lax' or 'that you bring about slowness and laxity.' آید expresses possibility/occurrence rather than physical motion.
segments.s651-g45: اگر is the conditional 'if.' جفت گردد is a passive/one-word verb phrase 'becomes paired' or 'is matched' (جفت = pair; گردد = becomes/is turned into). زبان بر دروغ literally places 'speech' upon/against falsehood (زبان = speech; بر = upon/toward; دروغ = falsehood), so the clause means 'if speech pairs/joined with falsehood.'
segments.s651-g46: نگیرد is the negative present/optative 3rd-person form of گرفتن (ن + گیرد) meaning 'does not seize / will not take.' In the poetical word order the verb appears before its modifiers: ز بخت سپهری فروغ = 'the radiance (فروغ) of celestial fortune (ز بختِ سپهری)'. So the clause says 'the radiance of heavenly fortune will not seize/take (it),' i.e. fortune's radiance will not attach to speech when it is false.
segments.s651-g47: سخن گفتن is a verbal noun/infinitive 'speaking' or 'speech' used as the subject. کژ is a predicative adjective 'crooked / perverse.' ز introduces the origin 'from,' and بیچارگیست (بیچارگی + است contracted) gives the source 'from misery/pitiable condition.' Structurally: 'Crooked speech is from misery.'
segments.s651-g48: به بیچارگان is a dative phrase 'for/to the miserable' (به + plural بیچارگان). بربباید is an archaic impersonal/modal form equivalent to 'must/ought to be' (it indicates obligation or that something should happen), and گریست is the infinitive/nominal form 'to weep' or the verbal noun 'weeping.' Together: 'one must weep for the miserable' or 'weeping ought to be for the wretched.'
segments.s651-g1401: چو is the temporal conjunction 'when'; مهراس is the subject (a proper name here). گفتار قیصر is a genitive/possessive phrase 'the speech/words of the Caesar' (classical juxtaposition instead of ezāfe), placed before the verb. شنید is the simple past 3rd-person singular 'he heard', so the whole clause reads 'When Mehrās heard the Caesar's speech...'.
segments.s651-g1402: پدید آمد is a two-word verbal idea 'appeared / came forth' (پدید = 'apparent', آمد = 'came', here functioning as the past verb). آن بند بد را کلید is a fronted noun phrase where کلید is the main noun 'key' and آن بندِ بد (literally 'that bad/evil bond') is marked by the object/topic marker را — classical word order can place the verb before the noun phrase for metrical reasons: 'the key of that evil fetter appeared.'
segments.s651-g1403: رسیدند is past 3rd‑person plural 'they arrived/came'. نزدیک here functions adverbially 'near (to)' and the epithet نوشین‌روان ('sweet‑souled' / pleasant‑minded) names the person approached. Classical Persian poetry often omits an explicit preposition, so this reads 'they came near the sweet‑souled (one)'.
segments.s651-g1404: چو is here a simile 'as/like'. الماس کرده is a participial phrase: الماس = 'diamond' and کرده is the perfect participle 'made' (literally 'having made into a diamond'), which adjectivally modifies زبان ('tongue' / 'speech'). با روان places the manner/adverbial 'with fluency/spirit' (روان here = 'flowing, fluent'). The phrase therefore describes a tongue 'made like a diamond, fluent in spirit'.
segments.s651-g1405: چو = 'when'; مهراس is the subject again. نزدیک کسری is the prepositional/locative idea 'near Khosrow (Kisra)' and رسید is the past verb 'arrived/approached'. The clause reads 'When Mehrās came near Khosrow...'.
segments.s651-g1406: برومی is an archaic verbal form here functioning as a light/performative verb meaning 'then proceeded to / went on to' (classical usage), introducing the action that follows. یکی 'one, a single' limits the act to a single instance; آفرین is an interjection/noun 'acclamation, "bravo"'; گسترید (past) literally 'spread' but here 'uttered/voiced' — together: 'he then uttered a single acclamation.'
segments.s651-g1407: تو is the 2nd‑person pronoun 'you'. گفتی here is the past 2nd‑person 'you said' (note: the provided dictionary senses listed conversational nouns, but context shows a verbal use). ز is the classical/prepositional 'from / by / because of'. تیزی = 'swiftness / keenness' and (وز) here functions as an archaic conjunction equivalent to و ('and'), linking تیزی and راستی; راستی = 'truth / uprightness'. The clause means 'You said that by swiftness and by uprightness...'.
segments.s651-g1408: ستاره is the subject 'the star' (a metaphor for fame/fortune). برآرد is a present/future‑oriented classical form 'will bring forth / will rise' (literally 'bring up'); همی is the continuous/emphatic particle giving ongoing/future force ('will / does'); زآستی is a fused phrase 'ز + آستی' meaning 'because/since you are (such)' or 'from your being' — together: 'a star will rise because you are so.'
segments.s651-g273: ز + noun opens the phrase with a poetic preposition 'from'. 'آنچ' is a relative/indefinite pronoun 'that which/whatever'. 'باید مدارید' is a literary obligation/imperative: 'باید' (ought) + مدارید (2nd-person plural/imperative or hortative of مداریدن) = 'you should spare/refrain'. The final adverb 'باز' modifies the verb (here sense of 'back/again' or 'away') and the whole clause leads into a justificatory 'که' clause that follows.
segments.s651-g274: 'که' here introduces a causal/explanatory clause ('for/because'). 'کردست' is an archaic contracted perfect (کرده است → کردست) meaning 'has done/made'. 'یزدان' is the agent 'God'. The next two pieces are the object 'مرا' split into 'م' + object marker 'را' (the dataset splits them): the intended meaning is 'me' as the patient. 'بی‌نیاز' is a predicate adjective 'free of need/self-sufficient'. The clause thus reads 'for God has made me self-sufficient.'
segments.s651-g275: 'چو' is the temporal conjunction 'when'. The verb and adjective 'ویران بود' ('was ruined') precede the subject 'بوم' in poetic word order (V‑Adj before noun). 'در بر من' is an idiomatic locative/benefactive phrase meaning 'before me / in my presence / for me' (here indicating the speaker's relation to the ruined homeland).
segments.s651-g276: 'نتابد' is a negative present/past literary form of 'تابیدن' here 'does not / did not shine/fall'. 'درو' is the contraction of 'در او' = 'on/in it' referring back to 'بوم'. 'سایه فر من' is a genitive phrase 'the shade/shadow of my فر' (فر = royal glory); the whole clause says that the protective/radiant 'shadow' of the speaker's glory did not shine on the ruined land.
segments.s651-g277: 'کسی را که' is a common poetic fronting: 'کسی' (someone) marked by 'را' (topic/definite marker) followed by the relative 'که' ('who/that'). 'باشد' here is a literary present/subjunctive copula meaning 'is/has' but the corpus senses provided don't match exactly (see senses mapping). 'برین' is the compound of 'بر' + 'این' (here meaning 'for/on this' → 'for this'); 'مایه کار' means 'the capital/means for the undertaking'. The whole unit names 'someone who has the means for this affair.'
segments.s651-g278: The conditional begins with 'اگر' ('if'). 'گیرد' is the literary present/subjunctive of 'گرفتن' used here as 'undertakes/takes on' (i.e. takes the work upon himself). 'این کار' = 'this work', 'دشوار' modifies 'کار' = 'difficult'. The final adjective 'خوار' ('humiliated/despised') functions as the resultive predicate referring back to that person; in Persian poetry the copula is often omitted, so the consequence is stated as an adjective.
segments.s651-g279: 'کنم' is the 1st-person singular of کردن used here as a periphrastic verbal root meaning 'I will cause/do' (future/intentional sense). 'زنده' is the object/complement 'alive'. 'بر دار' is the location phrase 'on the gallows' (دار = gallows). 'جایی که هست' is a relative clause 'in whatever place he is / wherever he may be' — together the clause expresses the speaker's threat to hang the person alive, in whatever place he is found.
segments.s651-g280: 'اگر' introduces the conditional 'if'. 'سرفرازست' is a fused form 'سرفراز + است' meaning 'is proud/exalted'; the suffix 'ست' indicates the copula. 'وگر' (و‌گر) is an archaic conjunction 'and if / but if'. 'زیردست' means 'a subordinate/lowly (one)'. The pair gives the sense 'whether he is high-ranking or lowly.'
segments.s651-g673: شنیدم is the 1st-person past of شنیدن ('I heard'). ز is the poetic preposition 'from' (classical variant of از) and introduces a source phrase. گفتار (speech, words) is the noun governed by ز, and کارآگهان is a plural/collective adjective/noun 'the informed (ones)' that functions as the possessor/specifier of گفتار (i.e. 'the speech of the informed').
segments.s651-g674: This clause reports the content heard. سخن ('speech, word') is the topic; هرچ ('whatever') is an indefinite/exclamative determiner modifying سخن. رفت is the classical perfect/past sense 'went forth/was uttered' (i.e. 'was said'). آشکار و نهان are a pair of adjectives 'manifest and hidden' describing the words; the sequence is a nominal/elliptical clause meaning 'whatever words were said, whether open or secret.'
segments.s651-g675: که introduces reported speech here ('that...'); گفتید is the 2nd-pers. pl. past 'you said'. ما را uses را as an object/indirect-object marker ('to us' / 'said to us'). ز کسری = 'about / concerning Kisra (Khosrow)'; چه باک is an exclamatory rhetorical question meaning 'what fear?' or 'why fear?' (i.e. 'why should we fear Kisra?').
segments.s651-g676: This is an elliptical pair of interrogative/exclamatory phrases. The first چه ایران بر ما is 'what/what is Iran to us?' where بر here means 'to/regarding' (classical use). The second چه یک مشت خاک supplies the ironic answer: 'what — a single handful of dust' (i.e. 'only a handful of dust'). The second چه is emphatic/exclamatory; یک مشت خاک is a contemptuous measure phrase 'a handful of earth/dust.'
segments.s651-g677: فراخست is a classical/descriptive present/predicative form meaning 'is wide' (fused adjective+copula). بیابان is the subject 'the plain/wilderness.' و links the balanced clause کوهش بلند where کوه-ش is 'its mountain' (کوه + possessive -ش) and بلند is the adjective 'high/tall.'
segments.s651-g678: This nominal clause characterizes the host: سپاه is the noun 'army/host.' از here has a partitive/constitutive sense 'made up of / consisting of' and is followed by در (here meaning 'in/with' marking the kinds of arms/means). تیر، گرز، کمند are listed armaments/instruments (arrows, mace, lasso). The verb 'is' is omitted (classical Persian often uses parataxis), so the phrase reads roughly 'the army is of/with arrows, maces and lassos.'
segments.s651-g679: همه ('all') quantifies جنگجویان ('warriors, fighters'). بیگانه‌ایم is the classical copular present 'we are strangers/foreigners' (بیگانه + -ایم 'we are'), so the clause means 'we are all foreign warriors' or 'all of us fighters are strangers.'
segments.s651-g680: A coordinated nominal clause: سپاه و سپهبد ('the army and the spahbed/commander'), نه is the negation 'not', زین is a classical demonstrative 'this' (here 'of this'), and خانه‌ایم is 'we/they are of the house/household' formed from خانه + copular ending -ایم; together they mean 'the army and the spahbed are not of this household/house.' (Note: the provided senses list did not include the demonstrative sense of زین, so that sense is indicated as unavailable.)
segments.s651-g969: تو‌گر is the conditional marker meaning 'if (you...)' (an older/poetic contraction of تو گر). شهریاری is a nominal predicate 'kingship/sovereignty' used here as 'a sovereign/king'. نه precedes من کهترم to negate the following clause; من is the 1st-person pronoun and کهترم is an adjectival predicate with the 1sg suffix (-م) meaning 'I am inferior/lesser.' The clause thus reads 'If you are ruler, I am not lesser.'
segments.s651-g970: همان functions as a predicative/adverbial 'the same' or 'equal(ly)'. با introduces an instrumental/associative phrase 'with' and is followed by a coordination: سر (literally 'head', here honor/position) و افسر (afsar as 'helmet' or crown in older usage) و لشکرم (لشکر + possessive suffix -م 'my army'). Word order places the predicative 'همان' before the accompanying instruments 'با ...' to mean 'I am equal, with ...'.
segments.s651-g971: چه here is an exclamatory/rhetorical 'what/why' (not a question-word asking for a factual answer). بایست is the modal 'must/ought to' and takes پذرفت (the verbal stem) as its complement; classical Persian often uses a past-stem form as a non-finite complement. چندین فسوس is the object: چندین = 'several/many a' and فسوس = 'insidious whisper/idle talk' (plural/collective). The whole clause reads 'What is there to have to accept many whispers?' i.e. 'Why accept many whispers?'
segments.s651-g972: ز is the poetic preposition 'from/because of' and introduces the cause: ز بیم = 'out of/because of fear'. پی here modifies پیل to indicate the elephant's approach/footsteps (a relational sense 'foot/track'), so ز بیم پی پیل = 'from fear of the elephant's track/advance'. آوای کوس is a coordinated cause 'and the sound/beat of the war-drum' (آوای = 'sound/voice', کوس = 'war-drum'). Together this gives the reason for the whispered talk.
segments.s651-g973: بخواهم is a 1st-person present/future-form of خواه (I want/I will ask) expressing desire/request; کنون = 'now'. از شما marks the source/recipient 'of you' or 'from you' (i.e. 'I ask you for...'). باژ و ساو are coordinated items after از شما; in context they behave like objects/names. The sequence is 'I now request from you Baj and Sāv.' (Note: these items can be read as nouns/charges or names; the language is compressed and names here are presented without extra particles.)
segments.s651-g974: که introduces a relative clause 'who/that'. دارد is the present auxiliary 'has/does' used here to form a habitual or ongoing sense ('are wont to' / 'do'). به پرخاش = 'in/with assault' (به marking manner/purpose), پرخاش carries the meaning 'assault/aggression' (not just a verbal reproach). با روم = 'against the Romans/at Rome' (با marking the opponent/target); تاو appears as an epithet/noun following the clause (here used in the sense 'radiance' or as a cognomen) and modifies the force or name of the action. The relative clause therefore means 'who make assaults upon the Romans.'
segments.s651-g975: به + تاراج + بردند is a verbal phrase 'they took to pillaging / they carried off in pillage': به marks the manner/turning-into ('to pillage'), تاراج = 'pillage', and بردند is the 3pl past 'they took/carried (away)'. یک چند چیز is the indefinite object 'a few things' (یک چند = 'some/several', چیز = 'thing').
segments.s651-g976: گذشت is a past verbal predicate 'it passed/went by' (here stating that the event occurred/passed). آن ستم = 'that oppression/injustice' (آن = 'that', ستم = 'injustice'). برنگیریم is a negated jussive/optic form meaning 'we do not accept / we will not assume/submit' (بر- prefix + نگیریم, 1pl; classical Persian can use this to mean 'we will not turn to/accept it'). نیز 'also/too' adds that they, too, reject it. The sentence links the earlier pillage to a refusal to accept the injustice.
segments.s651-g721: بگفتند is past-tense third-person plural ('they said') used here as the narrative reporting verb. با introduces the addressee/recipient ('to/with') in older usage, so 'با نامور شهریار' means 'to the famed king'; note the verb precedes the prepositional phrase, a common order in classical Persian narrative.
segments.s651-g722: که introduces a reported clause ('that...'). ما بندگانیم is a present-state copular clause ('we are servants') with بندگانیم in 1st person plural. با + گوشوار attaches as an instrument/attribute ('with an earring' / 'wearing an ornament'). The construction reports what they told the king.
segments.s651-g723: برآریم is a 1st-person-plural form meaning roughly 'we set/bring/let us set' (optative/collective action) used here to indicate their taking action. ازین سان is a fixed expression ('از' + این written ین + سان) meaning 'in this way' or 'thus'. که introduces the subordinate clause 'which the king said/ordered'; note the verb فرمود appears before its subject شاه (typical inversion in older prose).
segments.s651-g724: یکی باره is a compound meaning 'one time' / 'at once' or 'on a single occasion' (یکی = one, باره = occasion). و is the simple coordinator 'and/then'. نامور جایگاه is the noun phrase 'famed place/seat' with نامور qualifying جایگاه.
segments.s651-g725: وزان is a fused prepositional expression ('و' + 'زان') meaning 'and from that' or simply 'from that'. جایگه is 'place', and the clause places شاه (the king) as subject and لشکر (the army) as object; براند is the verb 'he led/drove' placed at the end (typical classical word order). Together: 'From that place the king led an army.'
segments.s651-g726: به هندوستان رفت is a straightforward past action: به marks destination ('to India') and رفت is the past tense 'he went'. و links the next clause; چندی here is an adverb 'for a while', and بماند ('he stayed/remained') is used to report the subsequent state—classical narrative often uses this present/subjunctive-form to describe past continuance.
segments.s651-g727: به فرمان is a prepositional phrase 'by/at the command (order)'. همه is the subject 'all/everyone'. پیش او آمدند literally 'came before him' or 'came into his presence', with آمدند in the past plural. The whole segment means 'at the command, everyone came before him.'
segments.s651-g728: به جان هر کسی is an idiomatic phrase meaning 'for the life of each person' or 'for the sake of each one's life' (به + جان = concerning life/safety). چاره‌جو is a nominal adjective 'seeker of a remedy' or 'one looking for a cure/solution', and آمدند is the past plural 'they came'. The segment says that each person came seeking help to save their life.
segments.s651-g817: برایشان (for them) is a prepositional phrase with an enclitic/object sense; ببخشود is an archaic past transitive verb form 'granted/forgave' placed before its subject (inverted Poetic order); شاه جهان is the subject ('king of the world') forming a title after the verb. The sequence is essentially 'برای‌شان ببخشود شاه جهان' = 'the king of the world granted to them.'
segments.s651-g818: گذشته is a noun 'the past'; شد is the auxiliary/verb 'became/was'; the locative اندر دل او نهان follows and places the state: 'hidden in his heart.' Together this reads 'the past became hidden in his heart' (i.e. he put it out of mind). Word order places the resulting adjective/state نهان at the end for emphasis.
segments.s651-g819: نوا خواست is a noun+verb cluster: نوا 'a cry/sound' as the thing wanted and خواست the past 'he desired/asked for.' The source phrase از گیل و دیلم دوصد means 'two hundred from Gilan and Daylam' (classical placement: the numeral follows the source nouns).
segments.s651-g820: کزان 'from which/from them' introduces the source just mentioned; پس here means 'afterwards/thereafter'; نگیرد is the negative present/subjunctive form 'does not/would not take' used in narrative to mean 'would not take' or 'should not take'; یکی is indefinite 'anyone/someone'; راه بد 'a bad/wrong path.' The clause means 'from them thereafter no one would take a wrong path.'
segments.s651-g821: یکی پهلوان 'a certain champion' (indefinite subject) is followed by نزد ایشان 'near/with them' (prepositional phrase of proximity); بماند is a classical present/jussive form of ماندن used here in narrative sense 'remained/stayed.' Word order is subject → adjunct → verb.
segments.s651-g822: چو introduces a temporal clause 'when'; بایسته شد is 'became necessary/required'; کار لشکر is a genitive phrase 'the army's business/task' (کار + لشکر); براند is the verb from راندن 'to drive/dispatch' (here 'set in motion' or 'sent forth') and completes the clause: 'when the army's task became necessary, he set it in motion.'
segments.s651-g823: ز گیلان 'from Gilan' is a poetic/prepositional source; به راه مداین 'on the road/toward Madā'in' gives the direction; کشید is a past form of کشیدن used here for movement 'he drew/marched' (i.e. advanced toward Madā'in).
segments.s651-g824: شمار و کران 'number and boundary/extent' forms a compound object describing scope; سپه را uses را as the definite-object marker attached to 'the host/army'; ندید is the past negative of دیدن 'did not see.' The clause means 'he did not see the number or the limits of the army.'
segments.s651-g769: که here is the relative/complementizer 'that/who'. از کوچگه (literally 'from the little hamlet') places the person’s origin; هرک is the literary contraction of هر که 'whoever'. یابید is an older/preclassical third‑person form of 'find' (used like past/subjunctive in epic style). خرد here is the noun 'wisdom' (used broadly, often meaning sense/courage). The clause means 'whoever from the hamlet found wisdom.'
segments.s651-g770: وگر is the conditional 'and if / but if'. تیغ دارند is a present / literary perfect sense 'they have/are armed with swords' (دارند = possess). مردان is 'men'; گرد functions here adverbially/postpositively ('around/about' or 'gathered'), so the clause reads 'and if the men were armed with swords' or 'if there were men with swords.'
segments.s651-g771: وگر again = 'if'. انجمن باشد uses باشد (literary subjunctive/present of 'to be') to say 'if there is/should be an assembly'. از اندکی means 'of/from smallness' → 'consisting of a few' or 'a small group'. The whole segment: 'and if there is an assembly of a few (people)'.
segments.s651-g772: نباید is an impersonal negative modal 'should not / must not'. که introduces the subordinate clause. یابد is again the literary form 'find' (subjunctive/infinitival sense after نباید). رهایی = 'escape/freedom'. یکی here means 'anyone / one person'. Together: 'no one should be able to find escape.'
segments.s651-g773: چو = 'when' (temporal). آگاه شد is 'became aware' (آگاه = aware; شد = became). لشکر is 'army/host'. از here means 'of/about' (introducing the cause or object of awareness). خشم شاه = 'the king's anger'. So: 'When the army became aware of the king's anger.'
segments.s651-g774: سوار و پیاده pairs 'mounted (troops)/cavalry' and 'infantry' (سوار = rider/horseman; پیاده = foot/infantry). ببستند is a past plural 'they closed/blocked' (3rd person plural perfect). راه = 'road/way'. The clause: 'the cavalry and infantry blocked the road.'
segments.s651-g775: از ایشان = 'of them / from them'. فراوان و اندک نماند uses نماند (negative past/imperfect sense 'did not remain / was not left') with the paired adjectives 'faraavan (many/plentiful) and andak (few/small)'; the poetic expression means 'neither great nor small remained' (i.e., none were left).
segments.s651-g776: This is a catalogue of those lost: زن و مرد جنگی و کودک — 'woman and man, warrior/fighter and child'. نماند (again) 'remained not / were not left'. The line lists categories to emphasize that absolutely no one remained.
segments.s651-g145: همه پادشاهان (subject) + شدند (past intransitive) + انجمن (predicate nominal). The verb شدند (past of شدن) here means 'became' in the sense 'gathered/assembled', so the clause literally reads 'all the kings became an assembly' (i.e., they met in council). Word order is subject → verb → predicate noun (a common poetic/orderly variation from modern colloquial word order).
segments.s651-g146: زمین را is the definite object (زمین + object marker را). ببخشید is the simple past plural of بخشیدن used transitively here meaning 'bestowed/gave'. و coordinates the next verb phrase. برزد is an archaic past-form verb applied to رسن (rope/rein); in poetic usage it can mean 'cast/let fall/struck' (an archaic sense), with رسن as its object. The subject of both verbs is the kings from the previous segment.
segments.s651-g147: گزیتی (name of the levy/tax or institution) is the direct object, followed by نهادند, past 3rd‑plural of نهادن meaning 'they set/established'. بر یک درم is a prepositional phrase 'at/on one dirham' specifying the rate. Word order (object + verb + prepositional phrase) reflects classical/poetic ordering.
segments.s651-g148: گر‌ای is the conditional particle 'if' (گر) possibly with an exclamatory/poetic linking vowel; دون is the noun 'base/ignoble (person)'. که introduces a relative clause 'who/that', دهقان is 'peasant', and نباشد is a negative present/subjunctive form 'is not' attached to that relative clause. دژم is an adjective 'sullen/grim' describing the antecedent. The whole structure reads as a conditional: 'if there be an ignoble one who is not a peasant (and is) sullen...'.
segments.s651-g149: کسی را is an indefinite noun with the object marker را used in a rhetorical/existential sense ('anyone / someone'), followed by کجا 'where/anywhere' for emphasis. تخم‌گر is a compound agent noun 'sower/breeder' and چارپای 'quadruped/beast of burden' is in apposition, listing both human and animal agents. This phrase is left without a finite verb here; the negative past verb comes in the next segment.
segments.s651-g150: به هنگام ورزش is a temporal adverbial 'at the time of (military/athletic) exercise' (به هنگام + ورزش). نبودی is past negative 'there was not' (third‑person past form used impersonally), and بجای functions adverbially 'in place' / 'to take the place'. Together they complete the existential meaning: 'there was no one/animal present to take the place at the time of exercise.'
segments.s651-g151: ز is the poetic preposition 'from'. گنج شهنشاه is a genitive phrase 'the king's treasure/treasury' (گنج + title شهنشاه). برداشتی is a past form meaning 'took/removed' (here expressing the action of taking from that source). Grammatically this is a short clause indicating the source and the action.
segments.s651-g152: وگرنه is a conditional 'otherwise'. زمین‌خوار is a compound noun 'one who devours/grasps land' (land‑grabber). بگذاشتی is a past/contrary‑to‑fact form meaning 'you would have left/you would have allowed' (classical past form used in a hypothetical/conditional sense). Together the clause gives the counterfactual: 'otherwise you would have left it to land‑grabbers.'
segments.s651-g1113: ز کار جهان آگهی داشتی is a compact past-state clause: ز (poetic preposition ‘from/about’) + کار جهان (a genitive phrase ‘the affairs of the world’) + آگهی داشتی (‘you had awareness/knowledge’). داشتی is the past indicative of داشتن used here to mean ‘you possessed/held’ (i.e. ‘you knew’). Word order is topical (prepositional phrase first) then the verbal predicate.
segments.s651-g1114: بد و نیک را خوار نگذاشتی: بد و نیک (the nouns ‘bad and good’) + را (object marker) + خوار (adjective ‘despised/worthless’) + نگذاشتی (past negative of گذاشتن ‘to let/allow’). The construction means ‘you did not allow [them] to be despised’ or more freely ‘you did not treat the good and the bad with contempt.’ خوار functions as a predicative adjective and نگذاشتی governs it; the object marker را marks the nouns as the patient of the verb.
segments.s651-g1115: ز لشکر کسی کو به مردی به راه is a noun phrase with an embedded relative idea: ز لشکر ‘from the host/army’ sets the source; کسی کو is an archaic/poetic way to introduce ‘someone (who)…’; به مردی ‘with/ by manliness’ uses به adverbially (‘by-ly’) + مردی (here ‘manliness’ rather than simply ‘man’); به راه ‘on the road’ gives the location. Word order is topical/source phrase first, then the relative/description.
segments.s651-g1116: ورا دخمه کردی بران جایگاه: ورا (‘to him/for him’ or ‘thither’) + دخمه (‘tomb/sepulchre’) + کردی (past of کردن ‘you made/did’) + بران جایگاه (‘on that place/at that spot’). The construction is SOV: object (vora + دخمه) precedes the verb کردی; بران جایگاه is a locative-demonstrative phrase specifying where the tomb was made.
segments.s651-g1117: اگر بازماندی ازو سیم و زر is a conditional clause: اگر ‘if’ + بازماندی (past form of بازماندن ‘to remain’ — here meaning ‘remained’ or ‘was left’) + ازو ‘of/from him’ and then the items left are listed (سیم و زر). بازماندی is the classical/poetic past tense; the implied sense is ‘if there remained of him…’.
segments.s651-g1118: کلاه و کمان و کمند و کمر is a simple coordinated list of items (helmet, bow, lasso, and belt) continuing as the catalogue of things that might have remained. The repeated و is the standard conjunction joining each noun.
segments.s651-g1119: بد و نیک با مرده بودی به خاک is a declarative past clause: بد و نیک ‘good and bad’ as the grammatical subject + با مرده ‘with the dead’ (a prepositional phrase) + بودی (past of بودن ‘were/was’) + به خاک ‘in/to the earth’ indicating location. The sense is that both good and bad accompany the dead in the grave/earth.
segments.s651-g1120: نبودی به از مردم اندر مغاک: نبودی is the negative past of بودن (‘you/it was not’), here meaning ‘you were not’ or ‘there was not’; به از (comparative particle) + مردم ‘people’ = ‘better than people’ or ‘superior to people’; اندر مغاک ‘in the pit/abyss’ gives the location. Together: ‘you were no better than the people in the pit’—a moralizing comparative against pride in life.
segments.s651-g537: پس is a temporal connector 'then'. آگاهی آمد is an impersonal past construction literally 'information came' = 'news arrived'; آمد is simple past of آمدن. به روم و به هند is a double prepositional phrase ('to Rum and to Hind') marking the recipients of the news.
segments.s651-g538: که introduces reported content 'that'. شد is past of شدن 'became/it happened'. روی ایران (face/visage of Iran) is the grammatical theme: روی = 'face/appearance' followed by the name ایران. چو introduces a simile 'as/like'. رومی here refers to the Romans/people of Rum; پرند is an older/poetic form (here used as a plural notion 'they fled/ flew' or figuratively 'the Romans took flight') forming the image of Romans fleeing — the simile modifies the state of Iran's visage.
segments.s651-g539: زمین را uses را as the definite/object/topic marker ('the land'). به کردار is a prepositional phrase 'in the manner/appearance of' (کردار 'manner/appearance'); تابنده is a participle 'shining' modifying ماه 'the moon' — the whole phrase means 'in the likeness/appearance of the shining moon.'
